newsletterForm.description: Get notified when I publish something new, and unsubscribe at any time.

labels.title: I build reusable product systems for the web.
labels.description: This portfolio is the public shell of a product-focused monorepo: shared platform packages, production-style demos, and case studies across admin, SaaS, commerce, operations, and content workflows.

labels.title: I build software like a product engineer, not a page assembler.
labels.description: My work sits at the intersection of frontend architecture, design systems, platform foundations, and pragmatic backend delivery. I care most about software that stays coherent as the product surface grows.

labels.title: Bring me in when the product needs stronger systems.
labels.description: I’m interested in work that benefits from clear frontend architecture, reusable platform foundations, and pragmatic full-stack product thinking.

labels.title: Start with the current constraint and the outcome you need.
labels.description: For roles, consulting, advisory, or platform modernization work, send the context, constraint, and result you need.

labels.title: Selected production work across commerce, publishing, SaaS, and operations.
labels.description: A curated view of production systems, platform improvements, and delivery work across business-critical web surfaces.

labels.title: Full-stack engineering for clearer architecture, better performance, and safer releases.

labels.title: The stack behind my product and platform work.
labels.description: The tools here are less about novelty and more about leverage: the hardware, frameworks, and workflow pieces I rely on to ship maintainable products with good developer ergonomics.

labels.title: Notes on frontend architecture, product systems, and delivery.
labels.description: Writing on the mechanics behind good product engineering: architecture, delivery systems, reusable UI, content modeling, and the tradeoffs that show up in real teams.
